content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Frank C. Damrell 
Damrell, Damrell & Nelson 
1625 I street 
Modesto, 
CA 
95354 
Dear Mr. Damrell: 
June 17, 1987 
Re: 
California- Collider Commission 
Our File No. I-87-158 
On May 22, 1987, the Office of the Secretary of State received 
an amendment to your firm's Lobbying Firm Registration statement 
(Form 601) adding the California Collider commission as an entity to 
which the firm will provide lobbying services. 
The amendment was 
forwarded to this office for a determination of whether the 
California Collider commission qualifies as a lobbyist employer. 
The Lobbying Firm Activity Authorization (Form 602) which was 
completed by the Chairperson of the Collider Commission's Executive 
steering Committee contained the following statement: 
This authorization is prepared for the sole purpose of ensuring 
that the lobbying firm registration statement required by 
California Government Code section 86104 is complete. 
California Government Code section 86300 exempts the Executive 
Steering committee as an entity of the State of California from 
all other lobbyist provisions of the Political Reform Act. 
section 86300(a) provides an exemption from the Act's lobbying 
disclosure provisions for: 
... any ~~loyee of the State of California acting within the 
scope of his employment .... 
(Emphasis added.) 
Employess of your lobbying firm are not employees of the State 
of California. 
However, the exemption for employees of the State of 
California may, under certain circumstances, apply to outside 
consultants who contract to provide lobbying services to a 8tate 
agency. 
Encl 
please find a copy of an advice letter issued to Sharon 
Quessenberry of the law firm Turner & Sullivan (No. A-84-121). 
The 
letter states that the exclusion in Government Code section 86300 
to a law firm and its employees who provide lobbying 
to a 
agency so long as each employee who provides 
to 
state agency is a "des 
(Government Code Section 82019) and files statements of 
interests 
the conflict of interest code which the agency is 
adopt (Government Code sections 87300 87313 . 
Suite 800 • 
P 
Box 807 • Sacranwnto CA 95804~0807 .. (916)32 


Frank C. Damrell 
Page 2 
Therefore, unless the employees of Damrell, Damrell and Nelson 
who will provide lobbying services to the California Collider 
Commission are designated in the Commission's conflict of interest 
code and file statements of economic interests, the Commission must 
file reports as a lobbyist employer. 
In addition, Damrell, Damrell 
and Nelson must report on its quarterly Lobbying Firm Report (Form 
625) the required information for a lobbyist employer in connection 
with the services it provides to the Commission. 
If you have any questions regarding the above, please contact me 
at (916) 322-5662. 
Enclosures 
cc: 
William B. Baker, 
Sincerely, 
{}~'-tAJ~ 
Carla Wardlow 
Political Reform Consultant 
Technical Assistance and 
Analysis Division 
California Collider commission 
Secretary of State

sections.analysis:
Division 
California Collider commission 
Secretary of State
sections.question_synthetic: Does the California Collider Commission qualify as a lobbyist employer requiring registration and reporting when Damrell, Damrell & Nelson provides lobbying services to it?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The California Collider Commission must file reports as a lobbyist employer unless the lobbying firm's employees are designated in the Commission's conflict of interest code and file statements of economic interests. The lobbying firm must also report required information on its quarterly reports.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: OCR document with some illegible sections (particularly page 2). No formal QUESTION/CONCLUSION sections present; content extracted from body text. File number I-87-158. Document references an enclosure (advice letter A-84-121) for precedent. Some OCR errors visible but core message clear.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Does the California Collider Commission qualify as a lobbyist employer requiring registration and reporting when Damrell, Damrell & Nelson provides lobbying services to it?

CONCLUSION: The California Collider Commission must file reports as a lobbyist employer unless the lobbying firm's employees are designated in the Commission's conflict of interest code and file statements of economic interests. The lobbying firm must also report required information on its quarterly reports.

embedding.summary: The FPPC provides guidance to Damrell, Damrell & Nelson regarding whether the California Collider Commission qualifies as a lobbyist employer under the Political Reform Act. The Commission is subject to lobbying disclosure and reporting requirements unless the firm's employees are properly designated and comply with conflict of interest code provisions.
